name: Alcala de Henares – circular via Peña Bermeja and Los Cerros Park
name_original: Alcalá de Henares – circular por Peña Bermeja y el Parque de Los Cerros
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcalá De Henares offers a diverse landscape, characterized by the gentle plains of the Henares River and the undulating terrain of Los Cerros de Alcalá. This region features a mosaic of habitats, including riparian zones, forests, and cultivated lands, providing varied scenery for cyclists. The city's green heritage extends to numerous urban parks and greenways, such as the Pasillo Verde del Camarmilla, which offer shaded routes. The area provides a mix of urban and peri-urban routes, ranging from river paths to more challenging trails within the hills. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Henares The most popular touring cycling route is Alcala de Henares – circular via Peña Bermeja and Los Cerros Park, a 20.3 miles (32.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural beauty of Los Cerros, offering scenic views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Herreras River Bridge – Underpass loop from Alcalá de Henares, a difficult 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route features river scenery and diverse landscapes, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Average Route of the Vuelta – Anchuelo Route loop from Alcalá de Henares, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá De Henares is defined by the Henares River plains, the hills of Los Cerros, and extensive urban greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides along riverbanks to more demanding routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Alcalá De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Henares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Alcalá De Henares?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find gentle plains along the Henares River, providing relatively flat and scenic rides. In contrast, the undulating terrain of Los Cerros de Alcalá presents more challenging sections with varied elevation. The area also features urban greenways and paths through a mosaic of habitats including riparian zones, forests, and cultivated lands.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Yes, Alcalá De Henares offers routes suitable for various skill levels, including easier options perfect for beginners or families. While specific easy routes aren't detailed here, the presence of river paths and urban greenways like the Pasillo Verde del Camarmilla suggests gentler terrain. Overall, there are 17 easy touring cycling routes in the area, providing accessible options.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Alcalá De Henares?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31487171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herreras River Bridge – Underpass loop from Alcalá de Henares&lt;/a&gt;, a 22.5-mile (36.2 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40372148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Henares – View of Santos de la Humosa loop from Alcalá de Henares&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 58.5 km with considerable ascents. In total, there are 57 difficult touring cycling routes to explore.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Henares are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959917119/alcala-de-henares-circular-via-pena-bermeja-and-los-cerros-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcala de Henares – circular via Peña Bermeja and Los Cerros Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.3-mile (32.6 km) route that explores the natural beauty of Los Cerros. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31751736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Average Route of the Vuelta – Anchuelo Route loop from Alcalá de Henares&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied rural landscapes.

Q: What natural attractions or scenic viewpoints can I find along the touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes often pass through or near significant natural features. The &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cerros_de_Alcal%C3%A1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros de Alcalá&lt;/a&gt; are a major draw, offering diverse habitats and scenic views shaped by the Henares River. The Henares River itself and its riparian zones are part of a protected Natura 2000 network, providing beautiful riverside scenery. You can also find viewpoints from various peaks in the area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecino Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4618274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce-Homo summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks to see while cycling in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Absolutely. Alcalá De Henares is rich in history. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, which holds historical significance. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6286099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls and Antiquarium of Alcalá de Henares&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3025123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Aldovea Palace&lt;/a&gt;. The city's historic center, a UNESCO World Heritage site, also offers cultural exploration opportunities.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcalá De Henares?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both river plains and hills, and the extensive urban greenways. The mix of natural beauty and historical sites also contributes to a rewarding experience for all levels of cyclists.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Alcalá De Henares?
A: There are over 140 touring cycling routes available around Alcalá De Henares. This extensive network provides a wide range of options, from easy rides to more challenging paths, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: Is it possible to access touring cycling routes using public transport in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Alcalá de Henares is well-connected by public transport, including a train station. Many urban and peri-urban routes, such as a 22-kilometer urban route starting from the train station, allow cyclists to explore the city and its outskirts. This makes it convenient to reach starting points for various touring cycling adventures without needing a car.

Q: Are there specific parks or green spaces ideal for cycling in the area?
A: Yes, Alcalá de Henares features several excellent green spaces for cycling. The &lt;p&gt;Pasillo Verde del Camarmilla&lt;/p&gt; (Camarmilla Green Corridor) is a large park with shaded areas and dedicated bike paths. The &lt;p&gt;Parque Histórico O'Donnell&lt;/p&gt; also offers pleasant, shaded routes. Additionally, the &lt;p&gt;Arboreto Mediterraneo Dehesa Del Batán&lt;/p&gt; in Parque del Vivero provides a natural setting to enjoy Mediterranean flora while cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcalá De Henares?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the diverse landscape of Alcalá De Henares, with its river plains and hills, suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling. Summers can be hot, especially in the plains, while winters might be cooler. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always advisable.

Q: Can I find bike-friendly cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific bike-friendly cafes are not detailed, Alcalá de Henares is a city with a vibrant atmosphere. Many routes connect with urban areas or pass through towns, making it likely to find cafes, pubs, and other establishments for refreshments. The city's historic center and various parks offer opportunities for breaks.

Halfway between Zulema and Ecce Homo, we find this beautiful viewpoint, with fantastic views of Alcalá de Henares

Alcalá is the birthplace of Cervantes, writer of Don Quixote, also famous for its old university. This lively city has Roman origins under the name of Complutum, but it was the Arabs who gave it its current name when in the year 850 they built the castle of A-Qalàt in Nahar.
